public_functions.behavior_description: Initialize the eGTM PWM for a 3-phase inverter using contiguous ATOM channels with complementary outputs and hardware dead-time. Algorithm: 1) Enable the eGTM module and enable/select the CMU clock so that CLK0 runs at the required frequency. 2) Configure the DTM to take its reference clock from the selected CMU clock and program rising/falling dead-time to match 1 microsecond as per the configured clock ticks. 3) Prepare the unified PWM configuration: set center-aligned mode, set the base channel to the first channel in the contiguous range, set the target PWM period for 20 kHz, and enable complementary outputs via DTM for each phase pair. 4) For each of the three phases (U, V, W), fill per-channel structures with: channel index (base then subsequent sync channels), initial phase (zero for center-aligned), initial duty values, pointers to the dead-time configuration, and output pin configuration (pin macros left as TBD where not yet validated; do not bind unverified pins). 5) Assign pin multiplexing for all verified PWM outputs using the generic eGTM PinMap API; leave placeholders for any pins marked TBD until validated. 6) Initialize the PWM driver with the main config and channel array, then start/sync all configured channels so they share the same time base. 7) Configure the period event interrupt on the base channel SR0, install the ISR on CPU0 with the required priority using the interrupt install API, and clear/set the SRC request as needed. 8) Initialize the module’s status fields: mark the CMU clock and DTM clock usage flags, clear the ISR missed counter and last error code, and set the enabled phase mask to indicate that all three phases are configured. The function stores the driver handle and channel data for use by runtime updates.

Update the three phase duties with a stepped ramp and apply immediately. Algorithm: For each phase index (U, V, W), check if (current_duty + STEP) is greater than or equal to 100%; if so, wrap that duty to 0%. After wrap checks, increment each phase duty by STEP. Prepare an array of the three updated duty values (as percentages). Call the unified PWM runtime API to update all configured channels’ duties immediately; complementary outputs are generated by hardware dead-time logic, so only the main leg duty values need to be provided. No phase or frequency changes are performed here; updates are applied for the next hardware update point as per immediate update semantics.
